title: How to Reset Your Active Directory / SSO Password
text:
Q: How do I reset my Active Directory or SSO password?
A: Windows: Step 1: Press Ctrl + Alt + Delete on your keyboard and select 'Change a password'. Step 2: Enter your current password, then your new password, and confirm the new password. Step 3: Click the arrow or press Enter to finalize the change. If you are unable to log in, you can also reset your password via the self-service portal by navigating to https://selfservice.company.com. macOS: Step 1: Open the 'System Preferences' from the Apple menu. Step 2: Click on 'Users & Groups', then select your user account. Step 3: Click on 'Change Password', enter your old password, then your new password, and confirm it. Step 4: Click 'Change Password' to save the new password. If you cannot access your account, visit the self-service portal at https://selfservice.company.com to reset your password. Remember, your new password must meet the company's security requirements, including a minimum of 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character.
